title: Hierarchical change-point regression models including random effects to estimate empirical critical loads for nitrogen using Bayesian Regression Models (brms) and JAGS
author: Roth, Tresch, et al.

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: The two presented ways of modelling change-point regressions including random effects, with JAGS (BCR_JAGS) and with the brm function from the brms [7] package (BCR_brms), resulted in similar estimated CLempN (Table 1), latter being slightly higher with the model (BCR_brms). The estimated CL from the model (BCR_brms) including the non-linearity and the random intercepts of β0 made the outcome more realistic compared to the underlying data (Fig. 5).

steps.title: Method validation
steps.instruction: In addition, a simple boxplot comparison (pinpoint method applied by [15]) of different groups of N deposition is suggesting a change in foliar N:P ratio between the first group of 0-5 kg ha-1 a-1 and the second group of 5-10 kg ha-1 a-1 (Fig. 6), which verifies the estimated CLempN of both models.Fig. 6Changes in foliar N:P ratio with increasing N deposition values. The group-wise boxplot comparison (pinpoint method according to [15]) highlights a suggested change in foliar N:P ratio between the first (0-5 kg ha-1 a-1) and the second group (5-10 kg ha-1 a-1).Fig. 6
